text: Croconic acid (4,5-dihydroxy-4-cyclopentene-1,2,3-trione), chemical formula C5H2O5, molecular weight 142.07, is a single-component organic molecular crystal and a prototypical proton-transfer-type organic ferroelectric. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pca2_1 (No. 29), with unit cell a = 8.7108 Å, b = 5.1683 Å, c = 10.9562 Å, Z = 4. Molecules are linked into chains by resonance-assisted O-H...O hydrogen bonds. Croconic acid shows a large room-temperature spontaneous polarization (about 21 microC/cm2 experimentally) arising from intermolecular proton transfer and charge redistribution along the hydrogen bonds. In this work, first-principles density functional theory (DFT, GGA) with the Berry-phase method computed a spontaneous polarization of about 22.3-24.7 microC/cm2 along the c axis, and a one-dimensional Hamiltonian with Monte Carlo simulation predicted a Curie temperature of about 756 K, with polarization stable to roughly 400 K. The crystal structure (CCDC 147324) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 293 K with an R factor of 5.49%, a density of 1.913 g/cm3, and a yellow hexagonal-prism habit. The ferroelectric-mechanism study was reported by Cai et al. in The Journal of Chemical Physics (2013).
property_ref_title: Ferroelectric mechanism of croconic acid: A first-principles and Monte Carlo study
